---
title: "00 · Introduction"
subtitle: "CBSE / NCERT Class 10 Mathematics (Board Year) · 14 chapters · The first board exam"
---

## What this tier is for

This is the **CBSE / NCERT Class 10** track — the first board year. It
walks the rationalised 2023 NCERT textbook chapter by chapter, with
extra weight on the **Board-Pattern Practice** in every lesson because
this year's marks go on a certificate.

Class 10 is the year **algebra, geometry, and trigonometry** all meet.
You learn how irrational numbers arise from divisibility, how a
quadratic equation hides three different solution methods inside it,
and how a single right-angled triangle controls the heights of
buildings and the distances of stars.

By the end of Class 10 you can:

- Apply the **Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic** and prove that
  numbers like $\sqrt{2}$ are irrational.
- Find **zeroes of polynomials** and use the **division algorithm**.
- Solve **pairs of linear equations** by graphing, substitution,
  elimination, and cross-multiplication, and decide whether they are
  consistent.
- Solve **quadratic equations** by factorisation and by the
  quadratic formula, and analyse the **discriminant**.
- Find the $n^{\text{th}}$ term and the sum of an **arithmetic
  progression**.
- Apply the **Basic Proportionality Theorem** and **similarity
  criteria** (AAA, SSS, SAS).
- Compute **distance, section formula, midpoint, area** in the
  coordinate plane.
- Define the six **trigonometric ratios**, apply the standard
  identities, and solve **heights & distances** problems.
- Apply **tangent–radius**, **two-tangent**, and **cyclic-quadrilateral**
  theorems.
- Compute **areas of sectors and segments**, and **surface areas /
  volumes** of combined solids.
- Compute **mean, median, and mode** of grouped data, including the
  classical formulas using cumulative frequency.
- Compute **theoretical probabilities** of equally likely events.

## The lessons

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[01 · Real Numbers] --> B[02 · Polynomials]
    B --> D[04 · Quadratic Equations]
    C[03 · Pair of Linear Equations] --> D
    A --> E[05 · Arithmetic Progressions]
    F[06 · Triangles - Similarity] --> H[08a · Trigonometry Intro]
    H --> I[08b · Trig Identities]
    I --> J[09 · Heights & Distances]
    G[07 · Coordinate Geometry]
    K[10 · Circles - Tangents]
    L[11 · Areas Related to Circles]
    M[12 · Surface Areas & Volumes]
    N[13 · Statistics]
    O[14 · Probability]
```

## Lesson list

1. [**01 · Real Numbers**](01-real-numbers/index.qmd)
2. [**02 · Polynomials**](02-polynomials/index.qmd)
3. [**03 · Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables**](03-pair-linear-equations/index.qmd)
4. [**04 · Quadratic Equations**](04-quadratic-equations/index.qmd)
5. [**05 · Arithmetic Progressions**](05-arithmetic-progressions/index.qmd)
6. [**06 · Triangles (Similarity)**](06-triangles-similarity/index.qmd)
7. [**07 · Coordinate Geometry**](07-coordinate-geometry/index.qmd)
8. [**08a · Introduction to Trigonometry**](08a-trigonometry-intro/index.qmd)
9. [**08b · Trigonometric Identities**](08b-trigonometric-identities/index.qmd)
10. [**09 · Some Applications of Trigonometry (Heights & Distances)**](09-heights-and-distances/index.qmd)
11. [**10 · Circles (Tangents)**](10-circles-tangents/index.qmd)
12. [**11 · Areas Related to Circles**](11-areas-related-to-circles/index.qmd)
13. [**12 · Surface Areas and Volumes**](12-surface-areas-and-volumes/index.qmd)
14. [**13 · Statistics**](13-statistics/index.qmd)
15. [**14 · Probability**](14-probability/index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note}
This page reflects the snapshot of `_curriculum.yml` at the time of build.
Lesson statuses there are the source of truth.
:::
